meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve Can't Finish Projects
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning your code. Master a professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your first production-ready app with confidence.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of scope management and feedback loops. By adopting a 'Client Workflow'—treating your own project like a paid contract with clear milestones and constraints—you transform from a hobbyist into a professional engineer who consistently delivers production-ready features on schedule.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: a fresh repository, a burst of caffeine-fueled inspiration, and the dream of a world-changing app. Two weeks later, the momentum fades, the code becomes a tangled mess of 'to-do' comments, and the project joins the graveyard of unfinished repositories on your GitHub profile. The problem isn't your talent; it's your process.

To move from student to senior developer, you must stop building in a vacuum. By implementing a formal 'Client Workflow,' you simulate the rigor of a professional agency environment. Using tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you will enforce constraints, automate documentation, and finally cross the finish line of a high-value project.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Stop feature-creeping. A real client project starts with a rigid scope. Define exactly what 'done' looks like for your first release.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior product manager. Review these 3 core features and define a strict MVP scope that can be built in 14 days, prioritizing functionality over aesthetics.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Create a project_spec.md file outlining the MVP features and technical constraints.'

steps.title: Establish the Architecture
steps.description: Before writing logic, map the data flow. This prevents the 'spaghetti code' that leads to project abandonment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a high-level architectural diagram and a file structure for a full-stack SaaS app using Next.js and Supabase. Ensure modularity for future scaling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Generate the folder structure and initialize the core configuration files based on the project_spec.md.'

steps.title: Implement Test-Driven Development
steps.description: Write your tests before your features. This provides a safety net that makes you feel confident, not overwhelmed.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write unit tests for the primary authentication and database CRUD operations defined in the MVP scope.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Run the test suite and fix any initialization errors in the base setup.'

steps.title: Automate Feature Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to handle the heavy lifting of boilerplate code so you can focus on the unique business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the API route logic for the user dashboard, ensuring it follows the security patterns defined in our spec.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Implement the dashboard API routes and integrate the database client.'

steps.title: Final Review & Deployment
steps.description: Treat your deployment as a final client handover. Verify everything works under production conditions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a code audit on the current repository. Identify any technical debt or security vulnerabilities before the final deployment push.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Run a final build check and deploy to Vercel/Cloud platform.'

pro_tips: Commit every 30 minutes; it creates a psychological 'save point' that prevents you from losing motivation.
Use 'Time-Boxing' to limit research; if you can't solve a bug in 45 minutes, pivot to a simpler implementation.
Document your 'why' in the README as you go; future-you will need this context when you revisit the code.
Keep your terminal clean; use Claude Code to summarize your progress at the end of every session.
Always build for the user, not for the tech stack. If the tech doesn't serve the user's goal, cut it.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, share your repository in our community channel to receive a code audit from a senior engineer.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your ideas die in a private folder. By applying this professional workflow, you'll gain the confidence to ship, the skills to impress employers, and the discipline to build anything. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
